title: Delhi Chana Chaat

ingredients.name: chickpeas (cooked or canned, drained and rinsed)

subs.name: boiled black chickpeas (kala chana) give a nuttier, earthier chaat with more bite

ingredients.name: tamarind (soaked, pulped; or use ready tamarind chutney)

subs.name: 2 tablespoons of pomegranate molasses thinned with a little water provides a similar sweet-sour base
a teaspoon of dates paste (blend 3 pitted dates with 2 tablespoons water) adds natural sweetness with extra depth

steps.text: If making tamarind chutney from scratch, strain the soaked tamarind pulp through a sieve, combine with jaggery and a pinch of salt in a small saucepan, and simmer over low heat for 5 minutes until slightly thickened. Cool completely. Alternatively, use 3–4 tablespoons of ready-made tamarind-date chutney from a jar, which saves time and tastes excellent.

Combine the drained chickpeas in a large bowl with chopped onion, tomato, and green chilli. Add chaat masala, roasted cumin, amchur, red chilli powder, and salt. Toss everything together so the spices coat the chickpeas evenly. Taste at this stage — the chickpeas should be well seasoned and pleasantly tangy on their own before adding the chutneys.

Pour 3–4 tablespoons of tamarind chutney over the chickpea mixture and toss to combine. Squeeze the lemon juice over everything. Add the mint leaves if using and the coriander leaves. Toss once more. Taste and balance: add more chaat masala for spice, more lemon for tang, or a pinch of sugar if it seems sharp.

Serve at room temperature or lightly chilled in small bowls or paper cups. Delhi-style chana chaat is eaten immediately — the onion and tomato should still be fresh and crunchy against the soft chickpeas. If preparing ahead, mix everything except the onion and tomato up to 2 hours in advance and add those just before serving.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chickpea → boiled black chickpeas (kala chana) give a nuttier, earthier chaat with more bite; tamarind → 2 tablespoons of pomegranate molasses thinned with a little water provides a similar sweet-sour base; jaggery → a teaspoon of dates paste (blend 3 pitted dates with 2 tablespoons water) adds natural sweetness with extra depth
